2606.26866.title: Fortress and Gatekeeper: Theorizing Transitive Trust in Third-Party Cybersecurity Risk Governance
2606.26866.paper_summary: Third-party vendors, such as analytics platforms, cloud services, identity providers, and software suppliers, are increasingly embedded in digital service delivery. While these arrangements enable scale and specialization, they also move customer data and security-relevant practices into environments that customers rarely see, select, or evaluate. This paper examines this problem through a document analysis of the November 2025 OpenAI-Mixpanel security incident. The incident serves as an illustrative case for showing how a security event in a vendor environment can become a governance and accountability problem for the focal organization that maintains the customer relationship. Drawing on organizational trust research and agency theory, the paper argues that third-party cybersecurity risk is both a trust relationship and a delegation problem. Customers trust the visible service provider, while the provider relies on vendors whose security practices are only partially visible and controllable. The paper develops the concept of transitive trust, where customer trust in a digital service depends on the security practices of vendors authorized by that service provider. It then presents the Fortress and Gatekeeper framework, which explains cybersecurity governance boundaries through trust and data flows rather than formal organizational ownership alone. The analysis develops four propositions concerning vendor integration, metadata exposure, vendor assurance, and data proliferation. The paper contributes to cybersecurity governance scholarship by explaining how delegated data processing creates customer-facing accountability and by identifying implications for vendor tiering, data classification, contractual design, continuous assurance, and data minimization.
2606.26866.paper_summary_zh: 第三方供應商，如分析平台、雲端服務、身份提供者和軟體供應商，正日益融入數位服務的交付中。雖然這些安排使得規模和專業化成為可能，但它們也將客戶數據和與安全相關的實踐移入客戶鮮少見到、選擇或評估的環境中。本文通過對2025年11月OpenAI-Mixpanel安全事件的文件分析來探討這一問題。該事件作為一個示範案例，顯示了供應商環境中的安全事件如何成為維護客戶關係的核心組織的治理和問責問題。本文借鑒組織信任研究和代理理論，認為第三方網絡安全風險既是信任關係，也是委託問題。客戶信任可見的服務提供者，而該提供者則依賴於那些安全實踐僅部分可見和可控的供應商。本文發展了傳遞信任的概念，即客戶對數位服務的信任依賴於該服務提供者授權的供應商的安全實踐。接著，本文提出了堡壘與守門人框架，通過信任和數據流而非僅僅依賴正式的組織所有權來解釋網絡安全治理邊界。分析發展了四個關於供應商整合、元數據暴露、供應商保證和數據擴散的命題。本文通過解釋委託數據處理如何創造面向客戶的問責，並識別供應商分層、數據分類、合同設計、持續保證和數據最小化的含義，為網絡安全治理的學術研究做出了貢獻。
2606.26866.author: Yijun Chen et.al.
